codeReview.summary: The commit implements most polish work (page transition, token-header flash animation, modal/tab/row animations, skeletons, and dev candle fallback), but it does not satisfy the feature contract requirement that chart updates come from WebSocket data.

issues.description: Feature contract expects chart updates with new data points from WS (features.json line 600), but PriceChart sources data only via useCandles(interval), which performs REST polling (web/hooks/use-candles.ts lines 44 and 84) and has no WebSocket subscription path.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission feature metadata references skillName 'frontend-ui-worker', but the worker reported that skill was unavailable in runtime and substituted 'frontend-worker'.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json:589 sets skillName=frontend-ui-worker; handoff 2026-03-31T13-13-36-814Z... lines 96-98 record deviation using frontend-worker because frontend-ui-worker was not found.

sharedStateObservations.observation: User-testing guidance still says chart line/crosshair assertions should be blocked unless candles are seeded, but this feature added a dev mock-candle fallback that makes those assertions executable without seeding.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/user-testing.md:44 says treat line-render/crosshair assertions as blocked unless seeded; web/hooks/use-candles.ts:26 and 51-64 now fall back to deterministic mock candles in development.

summary: Review failed due to one blocking gap: chart data is still REST-polled rather than WebSocket-driven as required by the feature contract.
